Reg. Affairs · Trainee
Training Environment
Personal study tool for learning eCTD structure, submission content, and FDA regulatory pathways. Does not transmit to FDA ESG and is not a validated eCTD compiler. Real submissions require validated tooling (Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTDmanager, GlobalSubmit) and FDA ESG credentials.

Tools · Decision Support

Pathway Decision Helper

Not sure which submission applies? Answer up to four questions to identify the appropriate FDA pathway.

Tools · Lifecycle

Drug Development Lifecycle

From discovery through post-marketing, with typical durations and regulatory milestones.

Small Molecule (NDA)
Biologic (BLA 351(a))
Biosimilar (BLA 351(k))
Generic (ANDA)
Tools · FDA Engagement

FDA Meetings

Strategic touchpoints with FDA throughout development. Each has a specific purpose, timing, and content expectation.

Drug (CDER) Meetings
Biologic Meetings
Biosimilar BPD Meetings
Tools · Special Programs

Designations & Expedited Programs

Programs that can shorten development time, accelerate review, or grant market exclusivity for qualifying products.

Reference · Library

FDA / ICH Guidance Library

Curated references organized by topic, applicable across drug, biologic, and biosimilar submissions.

Reference · Quality Control

Submission Checklists

Self-audit checklists by submission type and dosage form. Click items to mark them complete.

Oral Solid Dosage
Parenteral / Injectable
Biologic

Reference · Document Templates

Document Templates

Starting-point structures for common submission documents. Click any template to expand.

Reference · CDRH Pathways

Medical Devices & Combination Products

FDA medical device regulatory pathways across risk classes, plus combination drug-device products. Click any topic to expand.

Reference · Post-Market Safety

Pharmacovigilance & Materovigilance

End-to-end safety surveillance across drugs, biologics, devices, and combination products. ICSRs, aggregate reports (DSUR · PBRER · PADER), signal detection, risk management, and regulatory inspections.

Pharmacovigilance · Knowledge Check

PV Quiz & Exam Prep

Test your knowledge across 9 PV topics — ICSR, PBRER, DSUR, PADER, IND Safety, Device, REMS, MedDRA, Signal — at three difficulty levels each. 30 questions per level. Educational use only.

Regulatory · Knowledge Check

Regulatory Quiz & Exam Prep

Test your knowledge across 8 FDA submission pathways — IND, NDA 505(b)(1), NDA 505(b)(2), ANDA, BLA 351(a), BLA 351(k), DMF, and Devices & Combination Products — at three difficulty levels each. 30 questions per level. Educational use only.

Pharmacovigilance · Coding Reference

MedDRA Browser

Search the training MedDRA dictionary (~175 LLTs across 27 SOCs). Type a term — nausea, anaphylaxis, hypertension, neutropenia — to see the LLT → PT → HLT → HLGT → SOC hierarchy. Training subset only — verify in the actual MedDRA browser before any regulatory submission.

Reference · Worked Examples

Case Studies

Fully-worked example dossiers for each FDA submission pathway. Each case study shows realistic content for every relevant section — for study and reference only.

Educational Examples
All case studies use fictionalized sponsor names, product names, and study data. Drug names, mechanisms, and indications may be modeled on real classes but specific values, sites, batch numbers, and study results are illustrative only. Do not use any of this content in an actual regulatory submission.
Reference · Terminology

Glossary

Acronyms and terms encountered in FDA regulatory submissions.

Welcome, Deepak

Your personal regulatory affairs training environment.

i.
Build submissions across all major pathwaysIND, NDA (505(b)(1) and 505(b)(2)), ANDA, BLA 351(a), Biosimilar BLA 351(k), DMF — each with the correct eCTD structure and pathway-specific guidance.
ii.
Cover small molecules, biologics, and biosimilarsModule 3 adapts to your product class. Biologics get cell banking, comparability, and immunogenicity sections. Biosimilars get analytical similarity (Tier 1/2/3) and the totality-of-evidence framework.
iii.
Use the Pathway Decision HelperAnswer four questions about your product to identify the right submission type — useful when reviewing case studies.
iv.
Study lifecycle, meetings, and designationsVisualize discovery-through-approval timelines. Review every FDA meeting type. Study Orphan, Breakthrough, Fast Track, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, RMAT, and more.
v.
Reference materials at handFDA/ICH guidance library, dosage-form-specific checklists, document templates, and a 100+ entry glossary.
Saved
